README.md:62:The training code is written primarily to be run on TPUs but can also be run on GPUs.
README.md:79:# For TPU
README.md:80:uv pip install -U jax[tpu]==0.7.1
README.md:86:# Should output `Version: 0.7.1`
README.md:93:- dvruette/orbax (for checkpointing on TPU + HNS storage): https://github.com/dvruette/orbax/tree/fix-glob-step-path-on-hns
README.md:108:# recommended for local runs only: skip safety check for whether data/checkpoint buckets are in the same region as the TPU
README.md:117:#### TPU
README.md:118:For running the training code on TPU, it is recommended to use [`ray`](https://ray.io/) as a coordinator.
README.md:119:We will not go into details on how to set up a TPU cluster with `ray` here (see [here](https://docs.ray.io/en/latest/cluster/getting-started.html) for details), but the entrypoint for this case is the `main_ray.py` script.
README.md:121:An example command to launch a training run on a TPU cluster with `ray` is as follows (you can also set some of these environment variables in your ray cluster config):
README.md:125:    TPU_VERSION=v5p-8 \
README.md:126:    TPU_POD_COUNT=1 \
README.md:127:    TPU_ZONE=us-east5-a \
README.md:149:#### TPU
README.md:150:On a TPU Ray cluster, you can use the `eval_ray.py` script to run the evaluation.
README.md:155:    TPU_VERSION=v6e-8 \

## Verdict:
verdict: confirmed
confidence: 0.9
evidence:
  - file: "README.md"
    line: 62
    snippet: "The training code is written primarily to be run on TPUs but can also be run on GPUs."
  - file: "main_gpu.py"
    line: 7
    snippet: "SAVE_DIRECTORY = os.environ.get(\"SAVE_DIRECTORY\", \"outputs/diffusion_trainer\")"
  - file: "requirements.txt"
    line: 7
    snippet: "jax==0.7.1"
  - file: "README.md"
    line: 80
    snippet: "uv pip install -U jax[tpu]==0.7.1"
notes: "The repo is JAX-based (not PyTorch) but supports GPU via a dedicated `main_gpu.py` entrypoint and a `jax[cuda]`/`jax[tpu]` install split; single-GPU inference is feasible though TPU is the primary target, and there's no CPU-optimized path.

evidence.snippet: The training code is written primarily to be run on TPUs but can also be run on GPUs.

data.notes: "The repo is JAX-based (not PyTorch) but supports GPU via a dedicated `main_gpu.py` entrypoint and a `jax[cuda]`/`jax[tpu]` install split; single-GPU inference is feasible though TPU is the primary target, and there's no CPU-optimized path.
